topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Emotion and Control
topics.description: This cluster centers on how neural correlates and neural mechanisms support emotion regulation, anxiety, and cognitive control across development. It also includes work on functional connectivity, neural representation, and decision making in relation to attention, reward, and inhibitory processes.

topics.title: Brain Connectivity Dynamics and Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on how functional and structural brain organization relate across individuals, time, and conditions, with emphasis on dynamic connectivity, network architecture, and structure-function coupling. It spans resting-state and fMRI-based studies of human connectome variability, network dynamics, and hierarchical organization.

topics.title: Alzheimer Continuum and Brain Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on Alzheimer disease across the continuum from preclinical stages to dementia, with emphasis on cognitive decline, amyloid and tau, and risk-related biomarkers. It also links brain aging measures such as brain age and MRI-based changes to neurodegenerative progression and dementia risk.

topics.title: High-Resolution fMRI Method Development
topics.description: This cluster centers on optimizing high-field MRI and fMRI methods for improved BOLD sensitivity, resolution, and reliability. It includes studies of protocol optimization, cerebrovascular reactivity, resting-state connectivity, and population receptive field mapping.

topics.title: Transdiagnostic Neuropsychiatric Imaging and Risk
topics.description: This cluster centers on transdiagnostic psychiatric disorders, especially depression, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, and PTSD, with attention to psychopathology, cognition, inflammation, and genetic risk. It also emphasizes brain imaging and resting-state functional connectivity as tools for understanding shared and heterogeneous neurobiological patterns.

topics.title: Neuromodulation for Depression and Related Disorders
topics.description: This cluster centers on noninvasive and invasive neuromodulation approaches used to treat depression, especially major depressive disorder and treatment-resistant depression. It also includes biomarkers and physiological measures such as cortical excitability, functional connectivity, and TMS-EEG.

topics.title: Neurodevelopment Across Early Life and Preterm Birth
topics.description: Studies in this cluster focus on brain and cognitive development from infancy through adolescence, with particular attention to preterm birth and early-life vulnerability. Common outcomes include white matter development, neurodevelopmental trajectories, and later cognitive outcomes.

topics.title: Neurodevelopmental Trajectories in Youth
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neurodevelopmental changes during childhood and adolescence, especially in autism and ADHD. It highlights developmental trajectories, functional connectivity, cortical structure, and links to psychopathology and resilience.

topics.title: EEG and Brain State Changes Across Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on EEG/electrophysiology studies of sleep, consciousness, and related brain-state changes in humans. It also includes aging and disorder-related differences, especially across healthy aging, Alzheimer disease, and other neurological conditions.

topics.title: White Matter Connectivity and Aging
topics.description: This cluster centers on diffusion MRI studies of white matter structure and structural connectivity, with emphasis on tractography, tract profiling, and microstructural characterization. It also includes work on age-related changes, normative modeling, and disease contexts such as multiple sclerosis.

topics.title: Epilepsy Connectivity and Seizure Localization
topics.description: This cluster centers on epilepsy research using EEG, intracranial EEG, SEEG, and MEG to characterize connectivity and network dynamics. It emphasizes identifying epileptogenic and seizure onset zones across focal and pediatric epilepsy.

topics.title: AI-Based Brain MRI Segmentation and Parcellation
topics.description: This cluster centers on automated analysis of brain MRI, especially segmentation, parcellation, and quality control using deep learning and foundation model approaches. It also includes related tools and datasets for anatomical reconstruction and cortical organization studies.

topics.title: Lesion Networks in Stroke and Brain Injury
topics.description: This cluster centers on how focal brain damage in stroke and traumatic brain injury relates to symptoms, recovery, and clinical outcomes. It emphasizes lesion network mapping, structural disconnection, and prediction of motor, language, and cognitive deficits.

topics.title: Language Processing and Lateralization
topics.description: This cluster centers on neural mechanisms of language processing, especially in multilingual and bilingual speakers, with emphasis on fMRI findings and hemispheric lateralization. It also touches on aphasia, speech, and related language comprehension and production phenomena.

topics.title: Open Neuroimaging Data and Workflow Standards
topics.description: This cluster centers on open, standardized neuroimaging data handling and workflow infrastructure, including BIDS-based conversion, cloud-enabled platforms, and integrated tools for clinical and research datasets. It also reflects efforts to improve reproducibility, interoperability, and actionable analysis in network neuroscience and related neuroimaging studies.

topics.title: Parkinson Disease Progression and Brain Changes
topics.description: This cluster centers on Parkinson disease and related prodromal or early-stage features, including REM sleep behavior disorder, phenoconversion, hallucinations, and cognitive decline. It also highlights associated brain changes such as striatal and substantia nigra alterations and broader atrophy patterns.

topics.title: Brain Atlas and Imaging Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging, histology, and atlas-building approaches used to characterize brain structure and function across species. It includes MRI/fMRI, virtual histology, and multimodal mapping methods for constructing and analyzing brain atlases.

topics.title: Neural Mechanisms of Pain and Body Representation
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging studies of pain processing, including chronic and acute pain, pain perception, and central sensitization. It also highlights body representation and ownership-related network changes, with fMRI methods used to probe underlying brain mechanisms.

keywords: functional connectivity predict crave across substance use disorder
functional neural predictors of early alcohol initiation in emerging adolescence
topics.title: Functional Connectivity Predictors of Addiction Craving
topics.description: This cluster focuses on neural connectivity markers associated with craving, substance use disorders, and addiction-related behavior across alcohol, opioid, and cannabis contexts. It also includes predictive studies of early alcohol initiation and neural signatures of drug behavior.

keywords: deep neural network representational alignment predict behavioral performance
topics.title: fMRI Encoding and Decoding Models
topics.description: This cluster centers on fMRI-based models that decode, encode, and align neural representations with deep learning approaches. It also includes work on cross-subject prediction, individualized activation patterns, and multimodal integration for interpreting brain activity and mental states.

keywords: molecular signatures of cortical thickness alterations
topics.title: Normative Neuroimaging Biomarkers in Psychiatry
topics.description: This cluster centers on neuroimaging-based biomarkers and normative modeling approaches for characterizing cortical and structural brain alterations in psychiatric and neurodevelopmental conditions. It also includes work on spectral dynamic causal modelling and GABA-related measures linked to auditory hallucinations and schizophrenia.

keywords: probabilistic modeling for alzheimer 's disease cerebral localization use imaging genetics data
topics.title: Imaging Genetics in Alzheimer Brain Organization
topics.description: This cluster centers on imaging genetics approaches to Alzheimer disease, linking cerebral localization and gray matter volume change with human brain neurochemistry. It also includes neurotransmitter-related variation, receptor and transporter biology, and inter-individual differences in brain organization.

topics.title: Hormonal Influences on Female Brain Function
topics.description: This cluster centers on how menstrual cycle, hormones, and menopausal hormone therapy relate to female brain structure and connectivity. It also includes studies linking hormonal state to body odor and hippocampal connectivity using multimodal analysis.

topics.title: Neural Connectome Cohesion and Uniqueness
topics.description: This cluster concerns the organization of the neural connectome and how cohesive network environments may relate to decreased uniqueness. It appears focused on structural patterns in brain connectivity rather than a specific method.

keywords: large data drive multimodal power benchmarking
topics.title: Multimodal Power Benchmarking Tools
topics.description: This cluster centers on software and benchmarking resources for analyzing multimodal power data in neuroscience. The included phrases suggest a methodology-focused set of tools rather than a biological research theme.

topics.title: Network hub participation and percolation metrics
topics.description: This cluster centers on network hubs and their participation patterns, with attention to percolation-related measures. It also includes a data-driven probabilistic metric used to characterize these network properties.

keywords: multi scale dynamics of cognitive network engagement
topics.title: Multi-scale Cognitive Network Dynamics
topics.description: This cluster concerns how cognitive network engagement varies across multiple timescales, including slow trends and trial-level performance. It focuses on understanding temporal dynamics in neural activity related to cognition.

topics.title: Test Cluster
topics.description: This cluster appears to involve test content only. No substantive neuroscience topic terms are available from the candidate list.

topics.title: Cortical Folding and Brain Mapping
topics.description: This cluster centers on cortical folding and related efforts to map brain structure using the Julich Brain and genetic guidance. It also includes work associated with the ENIGMA Gyrification Group and pilot studies.

keywords: age related reorganization and plasticity of functional gradients in primary motor cortex
topics.title: Age-related Plasticity in Motor Cortex Gradients
topics.description: This cluster focuses on how aging affects reorganization and plasticity in the primary motor cortex. It centers on functional gradients and their age-related changes in motor system organization.

topics.title: Brain and White Matter Changes in Sleep
topics.description: This cluster centers on structural and integrity-related changes in the brain and white matter, with emphasis on sleep structure. It also includes findings relevant to breast cancer patient populations.

topics.title: Neural Networks and Personality in Healthy Subjects
topics.description: Studies of neural network structure in healthy subjects, with attention to how temperament personality traits relate to brain organization. The cluster appears centered on biological and psychological correlates rather than a specific method.

topics.title: Machine Learning Model Validation Methods
topics.description: This cluster centers on machine learning models and their evaluation in neuroscience research. It emphasizes validation strategies and related modeling procedures.

topics.title: Motor-Language Mapping in Autism
topics.description: Studies of how motor and language processing are mapped in autistic individuals. The cluster appears to focus on neurocognitive relationships rather than a specific experimental method.
